opp.ais_why:
• Feb 20, 2026 large workshop (41 attendees from AF + KLM) and the 11 Feb pre-workshop alignment focused on traditional S2C/S2P capabilities, use-case demos, and SAP ECC/PRD/Tradeshift/Basware consolidation. No AI-specific topics surfaced in pre-workshop notes; the pre-RFI phase is targeted for end of March then RFP in spring. The workshop content was canonical traditional procurement-platform consolidation, not AI transformation.
• Qualified stage at $1M (large enterprise eval with a mixed AF-KLM team in English/French). Product scope is purely traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier) ‚Äî no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze, no Merlin Intake. The complete absence of AI SKUs combined with traditional workshop content is doubly negative for AI relevance.
• Medium confidence: significant engagement evidence (41-attendee workshop) but complete absence of AI products in scope plus pre-workshop talk track focused on architecture / integration is decisive. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4. Would move UP only with a major scope expansion adding AI products.

items.quote: WS made, positive feedback... no comparative scorecard shared
CFO Comex on 5th of April to decide for RFP process
Email sent by Aatish to Michael (CPO)... Waiting for response from Michael

metrics.narrative: The business problem is clear and multi-dimensional: consolidate a fragmented legacy S2P stack (SAP ECC/PRD, Tradeshift, Basware) and modernise category management, sourcing and contracting, per the 20 Feb 2026 workshop which walked 10 buyer-defined use cases. Zycus pitched quantified ROI proof (Delta Airlines 6600% ROI, 80% reduction in sourcing project cycle time, 60% in transactional cycle time; typical 20% more spend under management) and the 27 Mar 2026 Next_Step frames an EY-supported ROI-under-12-months case. However, AFKLM has never stated its own numbers (target savings, headcount, spend-under-management uplift), so the value case remains vendor-asserted, not buyer-owned. Quantifying savings on 2-3 real AFKLM categories with their finance lead is the move.

champion.narrative: Benoit Thibaudon (Procurement Strategy & Services VP) is the closest thing to a champion: a personal contact of Renaud, he attended the Zycus event 30 Sep 2025, joined the Delta reference call 16 Jul 2025, confirmed RFP timing repeatedly, and called directly on 20 Apr 2026 to explain the postponement, saying Zycus is 'a very serious option'. But he 'did not open the door' when asked how to maximise the win, has not introduced the CPO or CFO, and has gone quiet since (15 Jun and 24 Jun emails unanswered). Engagement has narrowed to operational SPOC Franck Dewez, who is accommodating on scheduling (agreeing 30 Jun to a week-of-3-Jul call) but holds no decision authority. The champion is real but passive, cooling, and without proven access to power, so building a mobilised champion is a priority.

competition.narrative: This is a Hackett-influenced, four-way pre-RFP field: Ivalua, Coupa and SAP Ariba all ran parallel capability workshops (Ivalua 24 Feb 2026 per Next_Step 9 Feb), and the Hackett consultant is 'more pushing iValua, Coupa, Ariba' (Next_Step 16 Apr 2025), making Ivalua the strongest vendor threat. Ariba is the SAP-incumbent question (whether AFKLM keeps Ariba for Procurement given the S/4HANA path). No competitor was named in the readable 20 Feb Zycus workshop, so this run carries the competitive picture forward without re-ranking. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing/postpone option: the S/4HANA migration and airline financial pressure could defer the whole S2P project for years (Next_Step 9 Apr, 27 Mar 2026). Our wedge is agentic AI/Merlin differentiation plus the Delta airline reference, which Hackett is discounting.

identify_pain.narrative: AFKLM runs a fragmented, partly manual S2P landscape (SAP ECC/PRD, Tradeshift, Basware) with category strategy scattered across Excel and silos, and scope-clarity gaps after the Hackett Group transformation study (Next_Step 13 Jun 2025, 16 Apr 2025). Workshop questions confirmed operational pain: inconsistent category templates across 200+ buyers, complex multi-lot sourcing with exclusions and service-condition variability, contract risk/compliance review load on legal, and contract-utilisation blind spots. Pain owners are Benoit Thibaudon (VP Procurement Strategy & Services), Patrice Bonnafous (Head of Digital Process for Procurement) and Franck Dewez (Group Procurement Project Manager). The competing pain is the SAP S/4HANA migration, which threatens to defer the S2P project for years (Next_Step 9 Apr, 27 Mar 2026).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible because the deal is pre-RFP: no MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement channel has been discussed on any call or field, and no current-contract-expiration forcing function is recorded. Implementation-side mechanics were only referenced generically in the workshop (admin-owned template/taxonomy setup, ERP interfacing at go-live). Visibility here will only come once the September RFP defines the commercial and legal track; until then paper process is genuinely unknown, not a hygiene gap.

sources: SF Task 15 Jun 2026 Shared Space invite
economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with Michael Grootenboer (new at AFKLM since late 2024, named Decision Maker) and the CFO, whose Comex gate the week of 6 Apr 2026 was to decide the RFP go-ahead (Next_Step 16 Mar, 27 Mar 2026). Neither has appeared on a single call; the only touches are email (Aatish to Michael 9 Apr on the S/4HANA window) and 15 Jun Shared Space invites to the relevant stakeholder airline financial pressure the CFO controls whether this project is funded at all, and the Comex outcome was never captured, so the economic buyer is identified by role but wholly unengaged. Opening CPO/CFO access through Benoit Thibaudon or the EY/DXC partners before the September RFP is the highest-leverage gap to close.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a consultant-led, multi-vendor market scan ahead of a formal RFP: it explicitly stated on 20 Feb 'We are not in a RFP situation... looking at market capabilities', and ran parallel vendor workshops (Ivalua 24 Feb, Coupa and Ariba also in the rotation per Next_Step 9 Feb 2026). Hackett Group is defining the new S2P operating model, a pre-RFI phase was targeted for end of March, and the RFP moved to September 2026 with a decision by December and final arbitration after the RFP process (Next_Step 20 Apr 2026). The Comex gate governs whether the RFP launches at all. The process shape is known; the unknown is the internal go/no-go outcome and the criteria weighting Hackett will set.

decision_criteria.narrative: The 20 Feb 2026 workshop exposed the evaluation criteria as 10 buyer-authored use cases: templated category strategy with gen-AI category intelligence (Merlin plus optional Bero/smartcube), complex sourcing (multi-lot, partial/no-bid, weighted scorecards, supplier prequalification), contract management (AI redlining, clause/GDPR search, risk scoring, executive summaries, template comparison), and catalog/eProcurement (push-to-catalog, JCU/UNSPSC hybrid taxonomy, ERP interfacing). Buyer probes were substantive and integration-centric (taxonomy mapping, cost-deviation tracking across R2P, ERP synchronisation), showing the criteria weight real S2P depth and configurability. AI/innovation is a stated criterion tracing back to the 2024 AIRF I goal to address spend via agentic AI, though weighting versus traditional capability is not yet confirmed.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

The single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing/postpone option, since the S/4HANA migration and airline financial pressure could defer the S2P project for years (Next_Step 9 Apr 2026). Our wedge stays agentic Merlin AI plus the Delta airline reference, which Hackett is discounting.

The buyer's own 2024 AIRF I goal was to address spend via agentic AI, so the agentic narrative is under-leveraged versus the stated appetite.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: AIR FRANCE KLM — Qualified · $1M · closes 23 Mar

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 5 is carried by momentum 52 vs stage-expected 50; business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 9 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (5), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 9 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 3/Negative because scope is purely traditional S2P and the 20 Feb workshop was canonical procurement-platform consolidation, not AI transformation. Yet the buyer asked substantive gen-AI questions (category intelligence, model choice, contract AI) and its 2024 AIRF targeted agentic AI, so latent curiosity exists but is not driving the evaluation.

items.change: Delivered the 20 Feb 2026 capabilities workshop to 41 AF+KLM attendees across 10 use cases, positive feedback
Forecast category moved Best Case to Pipeline by John Woodcock
RFP postponed to September 2026 and close date pushed 22 Dec 2026 to 23 Mar 2027 per Benoit Thibaudon call
IT Procurement (Philippe Boronat) and senior IT buyer Kevin Meunier pulled into the loop; Shared Space invites to Grootenboer and Thibaudon
Franck Dewez agreed to reschedule the project status call to the week of 3 Jul

deal_movement.summary: Momentum thinned after a strong 20 Feb 2026 workshop: the RFP slipped again to September 2026, the close date and forecast were reset, IT procurement was added to the loop in June, and the live thread is now a status call with the operational SPOC.

Hackett Group is defining the S2P operating model and leaning to Ivalua/Coupa/Ariba.
RFP postponed twice to September 2026; project may be deferred for years behind the S/4HANA migration.
Airline financial difficulties and geopolitical pressure put project funding at risk.
SAP S/4HANA migration is a competing IT priority and a coexistence question for any S2P award.

items.text: Ivalua leads the consultant's shortlist: Hackett Group is defining Air France KLM's new S2P operating model while pushing Ivalua, Coupa and Ariba, all of whom ran parallel workshops in February.
The whole project risks a multi-year postponement behind the SAP S/4HANA migration and airline financial pressure, and the RFP has already slipped to September 2026.

The 20 February capabilities workshop with 41 attendees drew detailed, engaged questions across sourcing, contracts and catalogs with positive feedback, but no comparative scorecard came back to confirm where Zycus stands.
IT Procurement lead Philippe Boronat and senior IT buyer Kevin Meunier were pulled into the loop on 15 June, but neither relationship has been established yet.
The economic buyers, CPO Michael Grootenboer and the CFO, have still never engaged, and no pricing or budget has been confirmed.

Engagement has fallen to the operational SPOC Franck Dewez. A mobilised champion with power access is still missing.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 18 Sep 2023, ~1,020 days old; in Qualified since 28 Mar 2024 with no formal-evaluation submission dates recorded. Close pushed 22 Dec 2026 to 23 Mar 2027 on 20 Apr 2026; 263 days to close. RFP originally spring/June 2026, postponed to September 2026 with decision by December. CFO Comex gate (week of 6 Apr 2026) outcome unknown 88 days later. Buyer explicitly framed 20 Feb as a market-capability scan, not an RFP. A pre-RFP, consultant-led, four-way evaluation cannot defensibly complete RFP, selection, negotiation and contracting inside the current window; the date depends on an RFP that has not launched.
evidence: 20 Feb 2026 capabilities workshop (41 AF+KLM attendees), detailed engaged questions across category management, sourcing, contracts and catalogs, positive feedback per Next_Step 16 Mar 2026
Buyer opened 20 Feb workshop: 'We are not in a RFP situation. We are mainly looking at market capabilities', so the deal remains pre-RFP
RFP postponed twice: spring/June 2026, then September 2026 per Next_Step 20 Apr 2026
Close date moved 2026-12-22 to 2027-03-23 on 2026-04-20 (field history)
Forecast moved Best Case to Pipeline by John Woodcock 2026-02-19 (field history)
CFO Comex decision gate week of 6 Apr 2026 (Next_Step 16 Mar, 27 Mar), outcome not captured
No CPO Michael Grootenboer or CFO engagement on any call; only email outreach (Aatish to Michael 9 Apr re S/4HANA window)
Franck Dewez agreed to reschedule the status call week of 3 Jul (email 30 Jun 2026), the deal's only live thread
north_star_verdict.headline: Pre-RFP deal in Qualified stage for 2+ years, 263 days from close. Capabilities workshop went well 20 Feb but the RFP slipped again to September 2026, the CPO/CFO economic buyers stay dark, and the whole project risks multi-year deferral behind the SAP S/4HANA migration.

flags: Economic buyer dark: Michael Grootenboer and the CFO control the budget amid airline financial pressure but have never engaged, so open that access through Benoit Thibaudon or EY/DXC before the September RFP.

Consultant risk: Hackett Group is defining the S2P operating model and leaning to Ivalua/Coupa/Ariba, so activate the Hackett inside contact (via Viraat) and the EY/DXC partners to reshape criteria toward agentic AI.

Single-thread cooling: engagement narrowed to operational SPOC Franck Dewez while sponsor Benoit went quiet, so a pre-RFP PoC on real AFKLM categories is the highest-leverage way to re-mobilise the buying group and prove ROI before the RFP.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a stalled, pre-RFP, consultant-influenced deal: engage the dark economic buyers, neutralise Hackett's rival lean, and rebuild multi-threaded momentum with a proof-driven PoC.

competitive_position.summary: Carried forward without re-ranking (no competitor named in this run's readable call). Field is Hackett-influenced and pre-RFP: Ivalua is the strongest vendor threat via the Hackett endorsement and its 24 Feb workshop; Coupa and SAP Ariba also ran capability sessions, with Ariba the SAP-incumbent question given the S/4HANA path. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing/postpone option, since the S/4HANA migration and airline financial pressure could defer the S2P project for years (Next_Step 9 Apr 2026). Our wedge stays agentic Merlin AI plus the Delta airline reference, which Hackett is discounting.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is showing real AI (Merlin gen-AI category intelligence, autonomous negotiation agents, AI contract risk scoring and clause search) but as embedded features inside a traditional S2P story, and no AI SKUs are in scope. The buyer's own 2024 AIRF I goal was to address spend via agentic AI, so the agentic narrative is under-leveraged versus the stated appetite.

items.quote: one data core, no silos, one cut... a unified platform... developed by Zycus. No acquisition, no byproduct
items.criterion: Integrated single-suite S2P (one platform, no bolt-ons)

we plug Merlin intake... we have more than 45 agents today, and they are in production

items.criterion: Agentic / gen-AI depth as a core criterion
SAP / ERP and S/4HANA integration and coexistence

items.quote: Delta is not using our reporting tool but an external BI

customer_expectations_fit.summary: AFKLM wants a configurable, integrated single-suite S2P with real AI depth that coexists with SAP; Zycus demoed strong fit on the unified platform and complex sourcing/contracting, but SAP/S/4HANA integration and native BI/reporting remain exposed, and AI is shown as embedded rather than the headline.

hard.ais_why:
• Feb 20, 2026 large workshop (41 attendees from AF + KLM) and the 11 Feb pre-workshop alignment focused on traditional S2C/S2P capabilities, use-case demos, and SAP ECC/PRD/Tradeshift/Basware consolidation. No AI-specific topics surfaced in pre-workshop notes; the pre-RFI phase is targeted for end of March then RFP in spring. The workshop content was canonical traditional procurement-platform consolidation, not AI transformation.
• Qualified stage at $1M (large enterprise eval with a mixed AF-KLM team in English/French). Product scope is purely traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier) ‚Äî no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze, no Merlin Intake. The complete absence of AI SKUs combined with traditional workshop content is doubly negative for AI relevance.
• Medium confidence: significant engagement evidence (41-attendee workshop) but complete absence of AI products in scope plus pre-workshop talk track focused on architecture / integration is decisive. Defaulted DOWN at 3/4. Would move UP only with a major scope expansion adding AI products.
